funfield 0.1.0

    • Coffee Field Model: One Field, One Policy, One Run
    • Moderated mediation with `pathXMY()`: the coworker assertiveness ESJT
    • Notation: F, F_t, and the fZ_X.Y schema
    • Moderated mediation with `pathXMY()`: the work-overtime EXSJT
    • Moderated mediation with `pathXMY()`: the speeding EXSJT

Two-panel low/high-Z field diagrams for a moderated X-M-Y model

Source: R/plotPathXMY_ZLH.R
plotPathXMY_ZLH.Rd

Returns a patchwork composition of two plotPathXMY panels, one at a low value of Z and one at a high value. Each panel renders the field with coefficients collapsed at the chosen Z value (b1 + bZ * Z_value), so the moderation becomes visually concrete: edges thicken/thin, nodes shift color, and the implied total effect on Y shifts between panels. The two panels share a single color legend at the bottom.

Usage

plotPathXMY_ZLH(
  x,
  mediator = NULL,
  Z_levels = c(-1, 1),
  Z_label = "Z",
  panel_titles = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

x

A pathXMY or pathXMY_decompose return.

mediator, X_label, Y_label, Z_label, M_labels, X_shape, Y_shape, digits, scale_max, score_intensity_max, ...

Passed through to plotPathXMY. See there for details. In particular, X_shape = "rtTri" marks X as itself an imagined action (speeding / overtime EXSJTs) and Y_shape = "square" marks Y as a regular continuous outcome rather than an action likelihood.

Z_levels

Numeric length-2 vector of low and high Z values (default c(-1, 1), i.e. one SD below and above the mean for a z-standardized between-person moderator).

panel_titles

Optional length-2 character vector of panel titles. If NULL, panel titles are auto-generated using Z_label and the Z_levels values.

Value

A patchwork object combining two ggplot panels with a shared bottom legend. Auto-prints in interactive sessions and knitr chunks.

See also

plotPathXMY

On this page
